Declares the `enter_standby` function to handle standby mode with a specified reboot reason.

# Purpose
This header file declares the function [`enter_standby`](<#enter_standby>), which is used to transition a system into a standby state. The function takes a parameter `reason` of type `RebootReasonCode`, which specifies the reason for entering standby. The `NORETURN` attribute is conditionally applied to the function when the `UNITTEST` macro is not defined, indicating that the function does not return control to the caller in normal operation. The file includes dependencies on `system/reboot_reason.h` and `util/attributes.h`, which likely define the `RebootReasonCode` type and the `NORETURN` attribute, respectively. The use of `#pragma once` ensures the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `system/reboot_reason.h`
- `util/attributes.h`
